metric_name: Lawson Software founding year and market presence
metric_value: Over 20 years of core-business applications experience as of 1996; strong AS/400 midrange heritage
notes: Founded 1975 Minneapolis MN; strong AS/400 and open-systems portfolio.

metric_value: 4 Process Suites incorporating 20 predefined best-practice process models (AP Invoice Approval, Requisitions, Credit Check, Employee Review)
notes: Procurement, Supply Chain, HRMS, and a fourth suite included.

metric_value: Multi-tier product/market strategy combining best-of-breed workflow with integrated process-oriented GUI desktop
notes: Aberdeen calls it a 'one-two-punch' vs. Oracle, PeopleSoft, SAP.

metric_value: Internal development of Oracle Workflow via Cooperative Application Initiative (CAI); initial availability Q1 1996
notes: Prior partnership with Recognition International / BancTec for Plexus FloWare.

metric_value: Proprietary workflow in version 5.0: email-based, database-based (stored procedures/triggers), and planned third-party APIs
Moved from third-party Cascade (spring 1995) to internally-developed R/3 integrated workflow management system
notes: Cascade developed and sold by BancTec and ICS Deloitte.

metric_value: Process-oriented GUI with Lawson Toolbar and Intelligent Folder Tabs; C/C++ Win32; supports Windows 95 and NT
notes: 32-bit compiled version to release concurrent with Lawson Enterprise CSS v7.0.

metric_value: Three levels: Personal (OED agents), Departmental/Workgroup (OED + Departmental Workflow Engine), Enterprise (OED + Process Suites)
notes: Distinct competitive advantage vs. one-size-fits-all competitors per Aberdeen.

metric_value: MAPI-compliant email with embedded active/passive application objects; future VIM support in v7.0
notes: Application objects (e.g., journal entry, purchase requisition) can be mailed as icons.

metric_value: R&D working on OED release with Java-based Internet deployment; Aberdeen expects Lawson in CSS Internet vanguard
notes: Forward-looking; based on Aberdeen's understanding of R&D plans.

metric_value: Cross-domain workflow spanning Lawson and non-Lawson via plug-and-play APIs; supports Wang OpenWorkflow and FileNet Visual Workflow
notes: Aberdeen framework factor 1 of 3.

metric_value: Three deployment levels (personal/departmental/enterprise) matching diverse computing environments
notes: Aberdeen framework factor 2 of 3.

metric_value: Data capture of key process efficiency metrics; Rapid Process Development (RPD) methodology for continuous improvement
notes: Aberdeen framework factor 3 of 3; Aberdeen coins term 'Rapid Process Development'.

metric_value: Unstructured, user-defined process sequences; lowest complexity; suitable for knowledge workers
notes: Aberdeen classification of workflow forms (Table 1).

metric_value: Semi-structured process support for workgroups; intermediate complexity
notes: Aberdeen classification of workflow forms (Table 1).

metric_value: Highly structured, repeatable OLTP-grade processes; highest complexity and integration depth
notes: Lawson targets this level with production-ready OLTP workflow.

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of Lawson OED uniqueness
metric_value: OED's business-process perspective 'stands unique among CSS' and represents 'distinct market advantage'
notes: Aberdeen editorial judgment; strong promotional tone.

metric_value: Lawson should be on any prospective customer's evaluation list; uniquely qualified to compete across multiple market segments
notes: Noted also available on AS/400 with OS/400 V3R6.

metric_value: Lawson will escalate the client-server fray and could redefine the CSS value-added technology model; potential for significant competitive market advantage
notes: Forward-looking claim on Lawson's competitive trajectory.

metric_value: When OED with Java is delivered, Lawson could find itself in the vanguard of CSS suppliers able to fully exploit the Internet's business potential
notes: Contingent on delivery of Java-based OED.

metric_value: Lawson Software acquired by Infor/Golden Gate Capital for approximately $2 billion in 2011 after IPO in 2001; grew to 4,500 customers and $736M revenue
notes: Source: GoldenGateCap.com press release July 5, 2011; TechCrunch April 26, 2011. Lawson survived but never dominated; remained a mid-market specialist.

metric_value: Lawson did implement web-enabled delivery (Self-Evident Applications 1997, browser-based Insight II 1999) but did not become a CSS Internet vanguard leader
notes: Source: FundingUniverse Lawson history. Company focused on vertical markets (healthcare, retail) rather than broad internet-era leadership.

metric_value: PeopleSoft acquired by Oracle for $10.3 billion in January 2005 after hostile takeover battle
notes: Confirms Aberdeen's competitive landscape was accurate; major CSS vendors did consolidate.

metric_name: Predefined best-practice process models in Lawson v7.0 Process Suites
metric_value: 20 predefined models across 4 suites vs. competitors offering single enterprise workflow strategy
notes: Quantitative differentiator cited in the study.

metric_value: OED and Lawson Workflow available on AS/400 (OS/400 V3R6) in addition to Windows 95/NT
notes: Differentiator vs. open-systems-only competitors.
